transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling CareLine Health Navigation, this is Ava. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, um, I've had this sharp pain in my lower right side since this morning. It's getting worse.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Let me just pull up your file. I see here you're Sarah Kimble, date of birth March 4th, 1988, and you're on Lisinopril 10 milligrams and Metformin 500 milligrams for your type 2 diabetes and hypertension.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Uh, okay, yeah that's me. Anyway, the pain is like a 7 out of 10, kind of near my belly button now too.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. Is the pain constant, or does it come and go? Any nausea, fever, or vomiting?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: It's pretty constant, and yeah, I threw up once about an hour ago. I don't think I have a fever.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That combination, especially pain moving toward your lower right side with vomiting, could suggest appendicitis. This needs to be evaluated in person right away.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Oh no. Should I go to urgent care or the ER?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Given the severity and location, I'd recommend going to the emergency room now rather than urgent care, since this may need imaging and possibly surgery.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Okay, I'll head there now. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome, Sarah. Please have someone drive you if possible, and let the ER staff know your symptoms started this morning. Take care.
